Package com.wip.service

Class AuthServiceImpl

java.lang.Object
com.wip.service.AuthServiceImpl
All Implemented Interfaces:
AuthService
@Service public class AuthServiceImpl extends Object implements AuthService
Service implementation that handles user registration for the courier tracking system.

This class implements AuthService and orchestrates the complete user self-registration workflow. It validates uniqueness constraints on username and email, confirms that the supplied passwords match, encodes the plain-text password using a PasswordEncoder, and then atomically creates both an AppUser (with the USER role) and a linked Customer profile in a single database transaction. The newly registered user is automatically assigned the USER role and cannot self-assign the ADMIN role.

Since:
1.0
Version:
1.0
Author:
Rohith Varma K

  • Constructor Details

    • AuthServiceImpl

      public AuthServiceImpl(AppUserRepository appUserRepository, CustomerRepository customerRepository, org.springframework.security.crypto.password.PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder)
      Constructs an AuthServiceImpl with the required dependencies.
      Parameters:
      appUserRepository - the repository for AppUser persistence operations
      customerRepository - the repository for Customer persistence operations
      passwordEncoder - the encoder used to hash user passwords before storage

  • Method Details

    • register

      @Transactional public void register(RegisterDto registerDto)
      Registers a new user account along with an associated customer profile.

      Validates that the supplied username and email are not already in use, and that the password and confirmPassword values match. On success, both the AppUser record (with an encoded password and USER role) and the linked Customer profile are persisted atomically.

      The registration process performs the following steps in order:

      1. Checks that the requested username is not already taken.
      2. Checks that the requested email address is not already registered.
      3. Verifies that password and confirmPassword are identical.
      4. Builds a Customer entity from the registration data.
      5. Builds an AppUser with the encoded password and role "USER", linking it to the new customer.
      6. Sets the bidirectional relationship between the user and the customer.
      7. Persists the user (and cascades the customer save) in a single transaction.
      Specified by:
      register in interface AuthService
      Parameters:
      registerDto - the registration payload; must not be null
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if the username already exists, the email is already registered, or the passwords do not match
